texto: ...We never set eyes on Fatima or our dog or the city we had known ever again. Like a body prematurely buried, unmourned withpot coffin or ceremony, our hasty untidy exit from Jerusalem was no way to have said goodbye to our home, our country and all that we knew and loved.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Ghada Karmi: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “...We never set eyes on Fatima or our dog or the city we had known ever again. Like a body premat…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, memoir, never pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e reflexao.
